notes: e-Card era (ecard3) pack = 9 cards: 5 common, 2 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo, 1 rare-or-better. Confirmed by PSA Set Registry / collector documentation (Expedition/Aquapolis/Skyridge share this structure) and the e-Card series ADDED the reverse-holo slot (per task baseline; Legendary Collection introduced reverse holos to WOTC). Holo (rare-holo) rate in the rare slot ~1 in 3 packs (Flipside Gaming era review; long-standing WOTC standard). Crystal (secret-rare) cards: Skyridge has 6 Crystal cards available in BOTH holo and reverse-holo printings (unique to Skyridge). EliteFourum compiled 9 documented box breaks: 1.66 holo crystals/box AND 1.66 reverse-holo crystals/box average (small, acknowledged-inconclusive sample, high variance — one box had zero). 1.66/36 packs = 0.046 per pack. SLOT MAPPING: Common slot (x5)=common. Uncommon slot (x2)=uncommon. Reverse slot (x1): holo crystal lives in rare slot, reverse crystal lives here at 0.046 secret-rare; remaining 0.954 modeled 50/50 common/uncommon filler -> common 0.477, uncommon 0.477. Rare slot (x1): secret-rare (holo crystal) 0.046; of remaining 0.954, holo 1/3 -> rare-holo 0.318, non-holo rare 2/3 -> rare 0.636. Hit bucket = rare-holo + secret-rare. Vintage e-Card boxes carry a large sealed collector premium so EV/booster will sit well below box price — expected, not a rate error. Counted secret-rare across both slots = ~0.092/pack = ~3.3/box, consistent with 1.66 holo + 1.66 reverse = 3.32/box. Confidence medium: pack structure is solid/published; crystal rate is a community 9-box sample; holo 1/3 is era-average not a per-set study.
